message:
fix: replace unsafe unwrap() with defensive Option handling in aider connector

The `parse_chat_history` method used `path.file_name().unwrap()` to extract
the external_id from the chat history file path. While `file_name()` returns
`None` only for root paths (like `/`) or paths ending in `..`, this pattern
represents a potential panic point that violates defensive programming
principles.

Changed from:
  external_id: Some(path.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy().to_string())

To:
  external_id: path.file_name().map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().to_string())

This change:
- Eliminates the potential panic by returning `None` instead of panicking
  when `file_name()` returns `None`
- Maintains the same behavior for normal file paths (still produces `Some(...)`)
- Follows the same pattern already used on line 110 for workspace extraction:
  `workspace: path.parent().map(std::path::Path::to_path_buf)`
- Aligns with Rust's idiomatic approach of using combinators over unwrap()

Impact: Low risk, no functional change for valid aider chat history paths.
All 28 aider connector tests continue to pass.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -105,7 +105,7 @@ impl AiderConnector {
 
         Ok(NormalizedConversation {
             agent_slug: "aider".to_string(),
-            external_id: Some(path.file_name().unwrap().to_string_lossy().to_string()),
+            external_id: path.file_name().map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().to_string()),
             title: Some(format!("Aider Chat: {}", path.display())),
             workspace: path.parent().map(std::path::Path::to_path_buf),
             source_path: path.to_path_buf(),
